summary: Matched the Sandbag fighter source by replacing asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.s with src/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.c and a new ftsandbag.h. The PR also expanded Fighter-related struct knowledge in fighter.h, added missing function prototypes needed by the C translation, updated object lists to build ftsandbag from src instead of asm, added it to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED, and renamed Sandbag entries in asm callback tables to ftSandbag_OnDeath and ftSandbag_OnKnockbackEnter. The author stated all functions matched and the build was OK; there were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 220-line hand-written assembly file asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.s and replaced it with a 114-line C implementation in src/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.c.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.h with prototypes for ftSandbag_OnDeath, ftSandbag_OnLoad, func_8014FA30, func_8014FB78, ftSandbag_OnKnockbackEnter, func_8014FBA0, func_8014FBA4, lbl_8014FC20, lbl_8014FC24, lbl_8014FC28, and lbl_8014FC48.

Implemented Sandbag OnLoad behavior by copying two u32 values from ftData ext_attr into x2D8_specialAttributes2 via __memcpy, assigning x2D4_specialAttributes, setting fighter flag bitfields at x2228 and x2226, and calling func_8014FA30.

Implemented func_8014FA30 as a sequence of joint/bone linkage and rotation calls using FighterBone entries from fighter->x5E8_fighterBones, including calls to func_8000C1C0, func_8000C228, and func_8000C420 with float constants -1.57079637f and -1.50098311f.
Implemented func_8014FB78 to refresh Sandbag special-attribute pair values from fighter_data->ext_attr into fighter->x2D4_specialAttributes and return the first copied value.
Implemented func_8014FBA4 to check fighter->xE0_airState, call func_8007D7FC when the value is 1, then call func_800693AC with action/state ID 0x155 and func_8007EFC0 using lbl_804D6554->x5F0.
Kept several trivial Sandbag callbacks as empty C functions matching blr-only assembly: ftSandbag_OnDeath, ftSandbag_OnKnockbackEnter, func_8014FBA0, lbl_8014FC20, and lbl_8014FC24.
Replaced callback table references in asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s from raw labels func_8014F9CC and func_8014FB9C to named Sandbag callbacks ftSandbag_OnDeath and ftSandbag_OnKnockbackEnter.
Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h with UnkFlagStruct bitfields, FighterBone, xE0_airState, x5E8_fighterBones, and byte/flag fields around offsets 0x2224-0x2228.
Promoted several include/functions.h placeholders to typed prototypes: func_8000C1C0, func_8000C228, func_8000C420, func_800693AC, func_8007D7FC, and func_8007EFC0.
Updated obj_files.mk so ftsandbag.o is built from src/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.o instead of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.o.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftsandbag.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Changed MWCC_LD_VERSION in Makefile from 1.1 to 1.2.5.
smart_moves: Used small local placeholder structs, PairStruct and UnkFighterStruct, to express only the needed layout for ext_attr pair copying and lbl_804D6554->x5F0 without overclaiming larger unknown structures.
Converted repeated raw offset access for fighter bones into a FighterBone array abstraction, making the C code readable while preserving the indexing pattern from assembly.
Introduced bitfield access for x2226_flag and x2228_flag, allowing the rlwimi/stb flag writes from assembly to become direct assignments such as fighter->x2228_flag.bits.b2 = 1.
Kept unknown or not-yet-named routines as func_ addresses rather than inventing semantic names, while naming the two clear callback-table entries as ftSandbag_OnDeath and ftSandbag_OnKnockbackEnter.
Made the build-system switch complete by removing the asm object from obj_files.mk, adding the src object, and including the source object in the Epilogue unscheduled list.
Preserved blr-only assembly functions as empty C functions, a low-risk tactic for matching trivial callbacks.
Matched data movement from ext_attr with an explicit 8-byte __memcpy in OnLoad, reflecting that the assembly copied exactly two words into the special-attributes area.
decomp_lessons: For fighter decompilation, matching a character file often requires simultaneously improving shared Fighter struct layout; here Sandbag needed xE0_airState, x5E8_fighterBones, special-attribute pointers, and packed flag bytes.
Callback table entries can be renamed to meaningful ftCharacter_OnEvent symbols once the C functions exist, even if the table itself remains in assembly.
When only part of a large global or attribute structure is understood, a narrow temporary struct with filler and known fields can be safer than guessing the full type.
Fighter bone operations often compile cleanly when represented as indexed FighterBone arrays with separate x0_joint and x4_joint2 fields.
Empty C functions are the correct representation for blr-only callbacks and should not be padded with artificial logic.
Build matching for a migrated asm file requires checking all object-list surfaces, not just adding the C file: obj_files.mk, e_files.mk, and compiler/linker version settings may all matter.
Adding accurate prototypes for external helpers can be necessary to control call ABI and match generated code, especially for pointer and float arguments.
naming_conventions: Character lifecycle callbacks use ftCharacter_Event style names, shown by ftSandbag_OnLoad, ftSandbag_OnDeath, and ftSandbag_OnKnockbackEnter.
Unknown functions retained address-based names such as func_8014FA30, func_8014FB78, func_8014FBA0, and func_8014FBA4 when semantics were not established in the PR.
Unknown branch-label functions retained lbl_ address names such as lbl_8014FC20, lbl_8014FC24, lbl_8014FC28, and lbl_8014FC48.
Struct fields in fighter.h follow offset-prefixed names, e.g. x10C_ftData, x2D4_specialAttributes, x2D8_specialAttributes2, x5E8_fighterBones, x2226_flag, and x2228_flag.
Temporary unknown structs use descriptive-but-cautious names like PairStruct, UnkFighterStruct, and UnkFlagStruct rather than asserting final game semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used __memcpy for an exact 8-byte copy from ext_attr to special_attr_2 instead of manually assigning in OnLoad, likely to reproduce the assembly's two-word copy pattern and codegen.
Repeatedly reloaded fighter->x5E8_fighterBones into a local bones variable between calls in func_8014FA30, mirroring the assembly's repeated loads from offset 0x5E8.
Represented sdata2 constants lbl_804D9E70 and lbl_804D9E74 as literal floats -1.57079637f and -1.50098311f in C.
Represented lbl_804D9E78 and lbl_804D9E7C usage in func_8014FBA4 as 0.0f and 1.0f arguments to func_800693AC.
Matched simple blr functions as empty void functions.
Added external prototypes with concrete pointer, integer, and float signatures to avoid default or unknown call signatures interfering with matching.
Updated MWCC_LD_VERSION from 1.1 to 1.2.5, which suggests linker/toolchain version selection was part of making this source object build correctly, though the slice does not explain the rationale.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says: "All functions are matched and build is `OK`. Thanks to everyone who helped in the discord :)"
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that refine FighterBone, UnkFlagStruct, PairStruct, or the Sandbag special-attribute layout introduced here.
Search later changes for semantic names replacing func_8014FA30, func_8014FB78, func_8014FBA4, lbl_8014FC28, and lbl_8014FC48.
Investigate why MWCC_LD_VERSION was changed to 1.2.5 in this PR and whether that was broadly required for Epilogue-built source files.
Compare other fighter OnLoad implementations for the same x2D4_specialAttributes/x2D8_specialAttributes2 ext_attr copy pattern.
Search for subsequent prototypes or header moves for func_8000C1C0, func_8000C228, func_8000C420, func_800693AC, func_8007D7FC, and func_8007EFC0.
